work_order.objective:
Delivery strategy: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev
Stage 3/3: app-dev
Project: aetherion-maze / Aetherion Maze Games
Primary objective: Converge Aetherion Maze under one lead-owned path that runs creative-dev first, then game-dev, then app-dev, with one primary delivery target and no parallel rebuild drift.
Current project summary: Aetherion Maze review lane with Godot web export, legacy prototype references, and Hermes operator tracking.
Current tracking now: n/a

Stage contract:
Tighten the tooling, packaging, portal, and review loop around the chosen implementation path. Reduce operator ambiguity and make the artifact reviewable without ceremony.

Prior handoff context:
- creative-dev: Creative target frozen on the Godot 4 web export. Cozy HUD/theme, vignette, palette, and input cadence are now guardrails. Legacy HTML/Canvas prototypes are reference-only. Acceptance language is codified and a concrete handoff to game-dev is in place.
- creative-dev next: {'lane': 'game-dev', 'action': 'Re-export the Godot Web build to /godot-web/ per acceptance-guardrails, verify HUD theme application, icon crispness (18–20px), vignette ordering (below HUD), input pacing (no diagonals; steady cooldown), mobile D-pad ≥44px, and deliver a short QA note of verified guardrails and any deviations.', 'references': ['/home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/creative-dev/aetherion-maze/handoff-game-dev.md', '/home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/creative-dev/aetherion-maze/vision-freeze.md', '/home/james/Hermes/state/workspaces/creative-dev/aetherion-maze/acceptance-guardrails.md']}
- game-dev: Converged on the Godot 4 Web build and re-exported to the single target path. Adjusted HUD icon sizes to hit the creative guardrail (18–20 px → set to 20 px), kept vignette below HUD, gated noisy camera logs behind ?debug=1, verified export integrity, and spun up a local review route to preflight. No parallel rebuilds were created; legacy HTML/Canvas prototypes remain reference-only.

Required output:
- concise human summary
- machine-readable json block with summary, implemented, verified, assumed, blocked, risks, next_actions, artifacts, handoff_needed

delivery_model.strategy_label: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev

stage_guidance.operator: Own scope, freeze the delivery target, and keep the handoffs honest.
stage_guidance.creative-dev: Define the creative target, vision guardrails, and artifact acceptance language before implementation drifts.
stage_guidance.game-dev: Own the playable build path and converge the actual game implementation on the chosen target artifact.
stage_guidance.app-dev: Own the app, tooling, portal, and review-loop support needed to make the chosen artifact reviewable and repeatable.

handoff_contract: freeze one primary delivery target before downstream implementation expands scope
treat creative-dev as the vision and acceptance guardrail for downstream lanes
treat game-dev as the owner of the playable implementation path once the vision is frozen
treat app-dev as the owner of portal, tooling, packaging, and review-loop support after the build path is clear
require each lane to leave a concrete handoff for the next lane instead of sending the operator back into open-ended review

verification_required: run the smallest local verification possible before claiming success
work_order.project_now: game-dev: Converged on the Godot 4 Web build and re-exported to the single target path. Adjusted HUD icon sizes to hit the creative guardrail (18–20 px → set to 20 px), kept vignette below HUD, gated noisy camera logs behind ?debug=1, verified export integrity, and spun up a local review route to preflight. No parallel rebuilds were created; legacy HTML/Canvas prototypes remain reference-only.

structured_result.summary: App-dev tightened the review loop for Aetherion Maze by adding a root review portal, stabilizing the /godot-web/ target route via symlink, providing start/stop review server scripting with preflight health checks, and adding a packaging script that emits timestamped zips with SHA256. Verified that /, /godot-web/, and /godot-web/index.html return 200 via curl on two server implementations and produced a packaged zip.
implemented: index.html review portal at project root linking Play and Play (debug) for Godot Web, with legacy prototypes marked reference-only
Root-level symlink godot-web -> _archive_20260521/godot-web for stable canonical path
tools/review.sh for COOP/COEP server start/stop with automatic curl preflight
tools/package_web.sh for timestamped zips with SHA256 and size reporting
README.md updated with new quickstart and corrected prototype paths
verified: curl 200 OK for same routes on port 8011 using alternate server
Packaging created artifacts/aetherion-maze-godot-web-20260521T014103Z.zip with sha256 a5ff78f7b617b659b980bb4cfd1b98880965f4c63d32ec13e2650a3d13bd76ae and size 9080315
assumed: Using a symlink for /godot-web/ is acceptable for the canonical path and matches the single-target strategy
The ?debug=1 toggle remains the correct flag to enable diagnostics in the current build
risks: Symlink may not be followed by some non-Python static hosts; packaging script includes content so zips are unaffected
Future Godot exports in new dated dirs require repointing the symlink to maintain the stable route
next_actions: Confirm the portal UX and packaging naming are acceptable
Optionally add a script to repoint the godot-web symlink when a new export drops
If desired, mirror packaged zips to the app-dev workspace in addition to the project artifacts/
